characters.name: Mara
characters.personality: Returning home after seven years, Mara is conflicted but ultimately demonstrates a sense of responsibility and love for her family. She is introspective and struggles with past resentments but also exhibits quiet strength.
characters.arc: Mara initially returns with rehearsed coldness towards her father, but by the end, she commits to staying, indicating a significant internal shift from detachment to acceptance and responsibility.

texture.clothing: her father's old grey coat, one with a torn pocket

relationships.note: They have a long-standing, often conflicted relationship marked by silent understanding and fierce arguments, but also underlying care.
Her dying father, Tom, waited for her return; she struggles with past feelings towards him but ultimately cares for him.
Neighbour who offers support and observations about Mara and her family.

characters.name: Jack
characters.personality: Jack is stoic, outwardly gruff, and carries the burden of staying while Mara left. He expresses anger but also shows a deep, if conflicted, kindness and loyalty to his family.
characters.arc: No clear arc within this excerpt; he remains consistent in his conflicted but loyal demeanor.

texture.habits: smokes cigarettes, walks ahead of Mara

relationships.note: He resents Mara for leaving but also cares for her, as shown by giving her tea despite their argument.
He has been caring for his dying father, bearing the financial and emotional burden.

characters.name: Tom Ferris
characters.personality: Previously a steady and selfless man in the village, Tom is now frail and dying, but still holds onto hope for his daughter's return and expresses clear affection for her.
characters.arc: He is physically declining but experiences a moment of joy and relief at Mara's return, fulfilling his wish.

texture.appearance: big hands gone soft and useless, ruined face

relationships.note: Mara's father, who waited for her return and is deeply fond of her.
Jack's father, who Jack has been caring for in his illness.
Neighbour, Bess, has been looking in on him daily.

characters.name: Old Bess
characters.personality: A practical, observant, and subtly kind neighbour who doesn't mince words but shows genuine concern for the family.
characters.arc: No arc within this excerpt; she serves as a stable, helpful presence.

texture.habits: lets herself in without knocking, brings food
texture.routines: looks in on Tom every day

relationships.note: Neighbour who offers a frank assessment of Mara and her mother, and encouragement for Mara to stay.
Neighbour who has been caring for the ailing father daily.
